queries.term: ("machine learning"[Title/Abstract] OR "deep learning"[Title/Abstract]) AND (genomics OR proteomics OR "drug discovery" OR "computational biology")

papers.title: Trends in the Application of Multiomics Based on Machine Learning in the Development of Probiotics.
papers.journal: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Machine learning has emerged as an important analytical approach in probiotic research.
ML facilitates the integration of heterogeneous omics data for more systematic and quantitative probiotic development.

papers.title: A framework for identifying the polyploid complex in Rorippa (Brassicaceae): combining trait evolution, herbarium records, and machine learning.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Seed arrangement, petal number, and genome size exhibited clear interspecific differentiation.
Phylogenomic analyses based on chloroplast genomes further resolved species circumscription consistent with these traits.

papers.title: High MAP3K6 Expression in Spinal Cord Injury Tissues Enhances Neuronal Apoptosis: Insights from Multi-Omics Data Integrating WGCNA, Machine Learning, and Experimental Validation.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The analysis identified 4,621 differentially expressed genes between spinal cord injury (SCI) and controls.
MAP3K6 was found to be significantly upregulated in SCI and exhibited robust diagnostic efficacy as a potential biomarker.

papers.title: Genome characterization of cluster EK1 bacteriophages JessellCookie and MiamiPanther.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Bacteriophages JessellCookie and MiamiPanther were isolated from environmental samples.
They have a podoviral morphology.

papers.title: Unraveling the genome of Epidendrum fulgens: demographic history and gene family dynamics in a resilient Neotropical orchid.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The genome assembly from Epidendrum L., a perennial-terrestrial orchid species, consists of 12 chromosome-scale scaffolds representing the 12 pseudochromosomes expected for E. fulgens.
The genome contains approximately 77% transposable elements and 30,830 protein-coding genes with an N50 of 88.6 Mbp and L50 of 5.

papers.title: Possible cross‑talk between sarcopenia and obstructive sleep apnea revealed by WGCNA analysis and machine learning.
papers.journal: The aging male : the official journal of the International Society for the Study of the Aging Male
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study identified 20 shared common genes associated with the comorbidity of sarcopenia and obstructive sleep apnea (OSA).
LGR6 and ARRDC2 were identified as candidate hub genes for diagnosing OSA combined with sarcopenia.

papers.title: Post translational modifications as biomarkers of soil microbe responses to nano-pesticides.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Nano-pesticides can induce subtle disturbance in soil microbial function.
Post-translational modifications (PTMs) serve as functional biomarkers for nano-pesticide-induced microbial stress.

papers.title: Uncertainty-aware hybrid deep generative framework for robust and explainable drug discovery.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:

A novel hybrid computational framework combines Graph Convolutional Networks, Variational Autoencoders, and Uncertainty Aware Adaptive Multi-Objective Optimization-based Reinforcement Learning to generate drug molecules.

The proposed method prioritizes reliability by incorporating uncertainty awareness and explainability, reducing false positives and addressing challenges in drug discovery such as robustness and interpretability.

papers.title: Prosta-omics: machine learning-driven TPSA prediction and molecular modeling of ASPM-inhibitors for prostate cancer treatment.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Single-cell RNA sequencing of prostate cancer tissues enabled fine-grained clustering and differential gene expression analysis across malignant and non-malignant cell populations.
ASPM emerged as a statistically significant biomarker associated with proliferative tumor phenotypes, and was identified through integrative omics-to-therapy pipeline.

papers.title: Integrating ensemble machine-learning and fibril docking to discover potent, novel triazole-naphthalene tau-aggregation inhibitors.
summary_bullets: Here are the three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
The trained QSAR model achieves high accuracy in predicting IC50 values for human-tau aggregation inhibition.
The top-ranked candidates for blocking fibril growth, such as TND-9, TND-15, and TND-5, exhibit predicted potency and fall within the blood-brain barrier window.

papers.title: Cell-free chromatin state tracing reveals disease origin and therapy responses.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The cf-EpiTracing platform profiles histone modifications in cell-free DNA from plasma to deconvolute cell types of origin.
The platform enabled identification of primary diseased tissues, stratification of lymphoma subtypes, and detection of early-stage diseases or lesions.

papers.title: AI can write genomes - how long until it creates synthetic life?
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: miR-1290 serves as a potential biomarker for osteoporotic fracture and regulates osteoblast function by targeting KDM5A.
papers.journal: Journal of orthopaedic surgery and research
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
Serum miR-1290 levels decreased from healthy controls to osteoporotic fracture (OPF) patients.
MiR-1290 promoted osteoblast proliferation and differentiation, and was upregulated during osteogenic differentiation.

papers.title: Identification of metabolism and inflammation-related biomarkers and potential drugs for schizophrenia based on comprehensive bioinformatics and machine learning.
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: AlleleMiner: A long-read pipeline for gene-wise de novo allele phasing and variant detection in diploid citrus cultivars.
papers.journal: DNA research : an international journal for rapid publication of reports on genes and genomes
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The AlleleMiner pipeline phases diploid gene sequences directly from PacBio HiFi reads without relying on reference-based coordinate systems.
The pipeline achieved an average phasing output of 91.5% of single-copy genes across 18 citrus cultivars with a ∼30× HiFi depth providing stable recovery of heterozygous alleles.

papers.title: Linking protein aggregation and structural stability to predict pathogenic MYH7 variants via machine learning.
summary_bullets: Disease-causing variants in Myosin Storage Myopathy generally destabilize the MYH7 α-helical coiled-coil domain.
Pathogenic variants cluster in locally unwound regions of the coiled-coil dimer, promoting dimer destabilization and aggregation.
The RDSM-MYH7 machine learning-based predictor achieved superior performance compared to existing tools for assessing pathogenicity of MYH7 missense mutations.

papers.title: Genomics of Sable (Martes zibellina) × Pine Marten (Martes martes) Hybridization.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study found a broad spectrum of hybrid types with mosaic recombinant chromosomes in sable and pine marten samples.
Mitochondrial DNA introgression from sables into most pine martens indicates displacement of native pine marten mitochondrial sequences.

papers.title: Integrative chromosome-scale genome analysis of cupuassu provides insights into witches' broom disease resistance and expands genomic resources for Theobroma.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The genome of a witches' broom disease-resistant cupuassu genotype (C174) was assembled and compared to a susceptible genotype (C1074).
C174 exhibits unique genetic features, including tandem duplications, dispersed duplications, and transposable element insertions that are not seen in C1074.

papers.title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Advance Bioinformatics in Africa: Opportunities, Challenges, and Ethical Considerations in Combating Antimicrobial Resistance.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Artificial intelligence (AI) can reduce turnaround times for antimicrobial-resistant (AMR) prediction from days to hours by leveraging whole-genome sequencing-based models.
AI techniques, such as machine learning and deep learning algorithms, can automate analysis of large-scale sequence datasets, predict resistance patterns, and model infection transmission dynamics.

papers.title: Identification of Genetic Variants Among Breast Cancer Patients and At-Risk Individuals: A Cohort Study in Sri Lanka.
papers.journal: Breast cancer : basic and clinical research
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Variants were detected in several high- and moderate-penetrance genes including BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, BRIP1, and MRE11A.
Among these, BRCA2 mutations and a PALB2 frameshift deletion were classified as pathogenic germline variants.

papers.title: Bioinformatic Analysis and Experimental Verification Reveal the Protective Role of Baicalein Against LPS-Induced Endothelial Cell Dysfunction.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Baicalein (BAI) alleviates lipopolysaccharide (LPS)-induced endothelial cell injury.
BAI modulates inflammatory response and immune microenvironment through regulation of MIR3142HG, VCAM1, and PLCXD1 genes.

papers.title: Unraveling the Phylogenetic Signal of Gene Expression from Single-cell RNA-seq Data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Six strategies were evaluated for calling single-nucleotide variants from scRNA-seq data.
scAllele, Monopogen, and Monovar consistently returned phylogenetically informative genotype calls.

papers.title: A Real-Time, Programmable, and Multiplex Ligation Chain Reaction-MNAzyme Platform for Single-Nucleotide Variant Detection.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The LCR-MNAzyme system enables real-time quantitative monitoring of single-nucleotide variants (SNVs) with programmable and multiplex detection.
The system uses a universal MNAzyme-based reporter to quantify multiple SNV variants while suppressing background amplification through hot-start LCR.

papers.title: DoBSeqWF: a framework for sensitive detection of individual genetic variation in pooled sequencing data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Double-batched sequencing (DoBSeq) is a cost-effective method that uses 2D overlapping pool sequencing to enable individual-level rare variant detection.
DoBSeqWF is a Nextflow-based pipeline that processes pooled sequencing data from alignment through variant calling, filtering, and final variant assignment for efficient analysis.

papers.title: ANOMALY: a Snakemake pipeline for identifying NuMTs from long-read sequencing data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The ANOMALY workflow detects nuclear mitochondrial DNA segments (NuMTs) from long-read sequencing data with high accuracy.
The pipeline demonstrated a precision of 1.000, recall of 0.989, and F1-score of 0.994 on 50 simulated datasets.

papers.title: Dual Confinement-Enhanced Multiple Single Nucleotide Variant Detection at the Single-Particle Level.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A novel strategy was introduced for analyzing multiple single nucleotide variants (SNVs) at the single particle level.
The strategy integrates molecular and nanomaterial confinement to accelerate the kinetics of multiplex recognition processes.

papers.title: NGSTroubleFinder: a tool for detection and quantification of contamination and kinship across human NGS data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
NGSTroubleFinder detects cross-sample contamination in human whole-genome and transcriptome sequencing data.
The tool also identifies sample swaps and mismatches between reported and inferred genetic and transcriptomic sexes.

papers.title: MobiCT: a UMI-based circulating tumor DNA analysis pipeline.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The MobiCT pipeline detects ultra-low-frequency variants in cell-free DNA samples using unique molecular identifier (UMI).
The pipeline has three main stages: UMI deduplication, alignment to reference genome, and variant calling.
